title: Software Development Languages
description: Test your knowledge of programming languages, their origins, syntax, and ecosystems across 40 questions.

questions.text: Which language was created by Guido van Rossum?
questions.explanation: Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. Its name was inspired by the BBC comedy series Monty Python's Flying Circus.

questions.text: JavaScript was originally developed at which company?
questions.explanation: JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich at Netscape Communications in 1995. It was originally called Mocha, then LiveScript, before being renamed JavaScript.

questions.text: What does JVM stand for?
questions.explanation: JVM stands for Java Virtual Machine. It is the runtime environment that executes Java bytecode, enabling Java's 'write once, run anywhere' philosophy.

questions.text: Which language uses the .rs file extension?
questions.explanation: Rust source files use the .rs extension. Rust is a systems programming language focused on safety and performance, developed by Mozilla Research.

questions.text: What language is TypeScript a superset of?
questions.explanation: TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft. It adds optional static typing and compiles down to plain JavaScript.

questions.text: Which language was developed by Apple for iOS/macOS development?
questions.explanation: Swift was introduced by Apple in 2014 as a modern replacement for Objective-C. It is designed to be safe, fast, and expressive for Apple platforms.

questions.text: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language. It is the standard markup language for creating web pages and was created by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991.

questions.text: Which language is primarily used for statistical computing?
questions.explanation: R is a language and environment specifically designed for statistical computing and graphics. It is widely used among statisticians and data scientists.

questions.text: What keyword is used to declare a variable in Go?
questions.explanation: In Go, the 'var' keyword is used for variable declarations. Go also supports the short variable declaration operator ':=' for implicit type inference.

questions.text: Which language introduced the concept of 'nil' for null values?
questions.explanation: Objective-C uses 'nil' to represent a null pointer or the absence of an object. This concept was popularized in Smalltalk-descended languages.

questions.text: What does SQL stand for?
questions.explanation: SQL stands for Structured Query Language. It is the standard language for managing and querying relational databases, developed by IBM in the 1970s.

questions.text: Which language uses indentation as part of its syntax?
questions.explanation: Python uses indentation (whitespace) to define code blocks, replacing the curly braces used by languages like Java and C#. This enforces readable code structure.

questions.text: What is the file extension for a C# source file?
questions.explanation: C# source files use the .cs extension. C# was developed by Microsoft as part of the .NET initiative and was first released in 2000.

questions.text: Which language was designed by Bjarne Stroustrup?
questions.explanation: C++ was designed by Bjarne Stroustrup starting in 1979 at Bell Labs. It extends C with object-oriented features while preserving C's performance characteristics.

questions.text: What does PHP originally stand for?
questions.explanation: PHP originally stood for Personal Home Page when Rasmus Lerdorf created it in 1994. It was later backronymed to PHP: Hypertext Preprocessor as the language grew.

questions.text: Which language is Kotlin primarily used for?
questions.explanation: Kotlin is primarily used for Android development. Google officially endorsed Kotlin as a first-class language for Android in 2017. It runs on the JVM and is fully interoperable with Java.

questions.text: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It is the language used to describe the presentation of HTML documents, controlling layout, colors, and fonts.

questions.text: Which company developed the Go programming language?
questions.explanation: Go (also known as Golang) was designed at Google by Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, and Ken Thompson. It was announced in 2009 and focused on simplicity and concurrency.

questions.text: What is the correct file extension for a Java source file?
questions.explanation: Java source files use the .java extension. The file name must match the public class name inside it. Java was created by James Gosling at Sun Microsystems in 1995.

questions.text: Which language uses the 'fun' keyword to declare functions?
questions.explanation: Kotlin uses the 'fun' keyword to declare functions. For example: 'fun greet(name: String): String { return "Hello, $name" }'. This was a deliberate choice to make function declarations concise.

questions.text: What does OOP stand for?
questions.explanation: OOP stands for Object-Oriented Programming. It is a paradigm that organizes software design around data (objects) rather than functions and logic.

questions.text: Which language is used to write React components?
questions.explanation: React components are written in JavaScript or TypeScript. React uses JSX/TSX syntax, a JavaScript extension that allows you to write HTML-like code within JavaScript.

questions.text: What does LISP stand for?
questions.explanation: LISP stands for List Processing. It was created by John McCarthy in 1958 and is one of the oldest high-level programming languages, known for its fully parenthesized prefix notation.

questions.text: Which language is known as 'the mother of all languages'?
questions.explanation: C is often called the 'mother of all languages' because many modern languages (Java, C++, C#, JavaScript, Python, etc.) were heavily influenced by or derived from C.

questions.text: What is Dart primarily associated with?
questions.explanation: Dart is primarily associated with Flutter, Google's UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications. Flutter uses Dart as its programming language for cross-platform development.

questions.text: Which of these is a compiled language?
questions.explanation: C++ is a compiled language — source code is translated directly to machine code by a compiler before execution. Python, JavaScript, and Ruby are interpreted or JIT-compiled at runtime.

questions.text: What keyword does Rust use to define a function?
questions.explanation: Rust uses the 'fn' keyword to define functions. For example: 'fn main() { println!("Hello, world!"); }'. The short keyword reflects Rust's design philosophy of conciseness.

questions.text: Which language uses the => fat arrow syntax for lambdas?
questions.explanation: JavaScript uses the => fat arrow syntax for arrow functions (lambdas), introduced in ES6 (2015). For example: 'const add = (a, b) => a + b;'. C# also uses =>, but JavaScript popularized it at mainstream scale.

questions.text: What does API stand for?
questions.explanation: API stands for Application Programming Interface. It defines rules and protocols for how software components interact and communicate with each other.

questions.text: Which language was created at Bell Labs?
questions.explanation: The C programming language was created at Bell Labs by Dennis Ritchie between 1969 and 1973. It was developed alongside the Unix operating system and became extraordinarily influential.

questions.text: What is the primary use of Bash?
questions.explanation: Bash (Bourne Again Shell) is primarily used for shell scripting and command-line automation on Unix-like systems. It is the default shell on most Linux distributions.

questions.text: Which language is Elixir built on top of?
questions.explanation: Elixir runs on the Erlang Virtual Machine (BEAM). It was created by José Valim to bring Erlang's fault-tolerant, concurrent capabilities together with a more modern, Ruby-inspired syntax.

questions.text: What does IDE stand for?
questions.explanation: IDE stands for Integrated Development Environment. An IDE is a software application that provides comprehensive tools for software development, typically including a code editor, debugger, and build tools.

questions.text: Which language uses .kt as its file extension?
questions.explanation: Kotlin source files use the .kt extension. Kotlin was developed by JetBrains and first appeared in 2011. It is named after Kotlin Island, near St. Petersburg, Russia.

questions.text: What paradigm does Haskell follow?
questions.explanation: Haskell is a purely functional programming language. It features strong static typing, lazy evaluation, and immutability by default. It was named after the logician Haskell Curry.

questions.text: Which language is typically used for embedded systems programming?
questions.explanation: C is the dominant language for embedded systems programming due to its low-level memory access, minimal runtime overhead, and direct hardware control. It is used in microcontrollers and firmware worldwide.

questions.text: What does JSON stand for?
questions.explanation: JSON stands for JavaScript Object Notation. It is a lightweight data-interchange format that is easy for humans to read and write, and easy for machines to parse and generate.

questions.text: Which language introduced async/await for asynchronous programming first at mainstream scale?
questions.explanation: C# introduced async/await in C# 5.0 (2012), becoming the first mainstream language to implement this pattern. It was later adopted by Python (3.5), JavaScript (ES2017), and others.

questions.text: Which language is associated with the Django framework?
questions.explanation: Django is a high-level Python web framework that encourages rapid development and clean, pragmatic design. It was created by Adrian Holovaty and Simon Willison and released in 2005.

questions.text: What is the name of Rust's package manager?
questions.explanation: Cargo is Rust's official package manager and build system. It handles downloading dependencies, compiling packages, and distributing libraries via crates.io, Rust's package registry.
